articles.title: Chinese banks set for $41 million payday from chipmaker's $8.6 billion IPO
articles.summary: Six Chinese investment banks working on chipmaker CXMT's $8.6 billion IPO are set to take home at least $41 million in fees, company filings showed, in a ‌boost for an industry whose income pool has shrunk over the past half decade. The payout would bring the ‌year's total fees from mainland IPOs to about $684.62 million, gaining on last year's $984.75 million, LSEG data showed. That compared with a 2022 peak of $4.16 billion ​on IPOs from heavyweights including China Mobile and CNOOC.

articles.title: ExxonMobil Expands Nigeria Presence With $1B Usan Infill Project
articles.summary: XOM's $1B Usan Infill Project marks its return to drilling in Nigeria and adds 40,000 barrels per day within 18 months.

articles.title: Top Asian Dividend Stocks To Consider In April 2026
articles.summary: As global markets navigate a complex landscape marked by geopolitical tensions and fluctuating economic indicators, investors are increasingly turning their attention to the Asian markets, where stability in China and Japan's mixed performance offer unique opportunities. In this environment, dividend stocks stand out for their potential to provide steady income streams, making them an attractive option for those seeking to balance growth with income amid ongoing market uncertainties.

articles.title: CNOOC reports 11.5% drop in 2025 net profit
articles.summary: The company's net production increased to 777.3mboe in 2025, an increase of 7% compared to the previous year.

articles.title: Record Oil and Gas Output Fails to Shield CNOOC from Market Volatility
articles.summary: Chinese offshore giant CNOOC reported an 11.5 percent drop in 2025 net profit as a significant decline in realized crude oil prices outweighed record-breaking oil and gas production levels.
